reasoning: With Russia ending pipeline deliveries through Ukraine in January 2025, the remaining active supply routes for Russian gas to the EU are essentially limited to the TurkStream pipeline (~15.75 bcm/year capacity to Europe) and Russian LNG shipments. Physical constraints put the maximum theoretical supply from these two sources combined at around 9 to 11 bcm per quarter. Even in 2023 and 2024, when Ukraine transit was still operational, total quarterly EU imports from Russia peaked around 11-13 bcm and never hit 15 bcm. Given these hard infrastructure limits, plummeting base rates, and the EU's continued commitment to the REPowerEU goals, reaching 15 bcm in any quarter of 2025 is physically extremely unlikely without the unexpected restoration of Ukraine transit or other heavily sanctioned pipelines, which is deeply improbable given the current geopolitical landscape.
